message:
chore(beads): file 3 conformance-harness findings (47bag, 8ud1m, y87ra)

/testing-conformance-harnesses run — differential testing against
upstream nx on graph operators, flow/cut family, and DAG algorithms.
Surfaced:

- 47bag (P1): all 5 binary graph operators (compose/union/intersection/
  difference/symmetric_difference) return plain undirected Graph
  regardless of input class, dropping DiGraph/MultiGraph identity.
  compose + union additionally drop node and edge attribute dicts.
  compose on MultiGraph crashes outright.
- 8ud1m (P1): edge_connectivity(G, s, t) returns the capacity-weighted
  max-flow value instead of edge cardinality when the graph has a
  capacity edge attribute.
- y87ra (P3): immediate_dominators(G, start) includes {start: start}
  in the returned dict; upstream nx excludes the start key.

Scan also passed: BFS/DFS traversal family (120 comparisons, 0
divergences), link prediction (9 comparisons, 0 divergences), clique
family, boundary/isolate, transitive closure/reduction, density,
symmetric_difference attr handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"franken_networkx-43ek","title":"Add no-fallback parity probe for all-pairs node connectivity","description":"Strengthen the existing all_pairs_node_connectivity parity test by computing the NetworkX oracle first, then patching the NetworkX symbol during the FrankenNetworkX call to catch runtime fallback.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T07:48:38.440007273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T07:49:31.443798522Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T07:49:31.441799148Z","close_reason":"Completed no-fallback all-pairs node connectivity parity probe","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-44kj","title":"parity: DiGraph edge_subgraph should preserve NetworkX node iteration order","description":"DiGraph.edge_subgraph(...) currently reverses the observable node iteration order in FrankenNetworkX. On a graph with edges a->b and b->c, H = G.edge_subgraph([('a','b')]) yields list(H) == ['b', 'a'] in FrankenNetworkX while upstream NetworkX yields ['a', 'b']; selecting [('b','c')] yields ['c', 'b'] instead of ['b', 'c']. Match the upstream node-order contract on DiGraph edge_subgraph views.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:23:47.805044067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:04.751029943Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:40:47.484104221Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"franken_networkx-44kj","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fixed in 9ba66ef (test gate) on top of 62b6f1d (tt28). DiGraph.edge_subgraph now routes through _multigraph_edge_subgraph → filtered view that iterates in original insertion order, not the reversed Rust order. Parity test covers both edge selections and a multi-edge combined selection.","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-4587","title":"NX-REPLACE: Centrality extensions — load, percolation, second_order, group, subset variants","description":"## Functions (10) — centrality algorithm extensions
1. load_centrality (__init__.py:3227) — like betweenness but counts all shortest paths through node
2. percolation_centrality (__init__.py:5762) — requires node percolation states
3. second_order_centrality (__init__.py:5776) — random walk variance
4. group_betweenness_centrality (__init__.py:5839) — betweenness for a GROUP of nodes
5. group_closeness_centrality (__init__.py:5850) — closeness for a group
6. betweenness_centrality_subset (__init__.py:5720) — betweenness restricted to source/target subsets
7. edge_betweenness_centrality_subset (__init__.py:5729) — edge variant of above
8. information_centrality (__init__.py:5771) — current-flow based
9. edge_load_centrality (__init__.py:5738) — load per edge
10. communicability_betweenness_centrality (__init__.py:5790) — matrix exponential based

## Implementation tiers
Tier A (reuse existing infrastructure):
- load_centrality: variant of betweenness counting, shares shortest-path code
- group_betweenness/closeness: extend existing single-node centrality with group aggregation
- subset variants: restrict existing betweenness to source/target subsets

Tier B (new algorithms):
- percolation_centrality: needs node state handling + modified shortest path
- second_order_centrality: random walk standard deviation computation
- information_centrality: current-flow based (needs pseudo-inverse or LU solve)

Tier C (numpy-dependent, consider keeping NX):
- communicability_betweenness: matrix exponential — may keep as NX

## Testing requirements
Create tests/python/test_centrality_extensions_parity.py:
- test_load_centrality_matches_nx: compare on 3+ graphs
- test_load_centrality_normalized: verify sum == 1.0 when normalized
- test_percolation_centrality_matches_nx: set states, compare
- test_second_order_centrality_matches_nx: compare on connected graph
- test_group_betweenness_matches_nx: group={0,1}, compare value
- test_group_closeness_matches_nx: group={0,1}, compare value
- test_betweenness_subset_matches_nx: sources={0}, targets={3}, compare
- test_edge_betweenness_subset_matches_nx: same setup
- test_information_centrality_matches_nx: compare on small connected graph
- test_edge_load_centrality_matches_nx: compare edge values

Each test: build graph, run both fnx and nx, assert values within 1e-10.
Run: pytest tests/python/test_centrality_extensions_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs: implement 7-9 centrality variants
- crates/fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs: add bindings
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewire functions
- tests/python/test_centrality_extensions_parity.py: new test file","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:13.447751417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:36.603155844Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:36.602760637Z","close_reason":"Completed centrality wrapper/native tranche; rewired safe Rust-backed paths, fixed percolation_centrality attribute parity, and split remaining algorithms to franken_networkx-1gmi","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-47bag","title":"All 5 graph operators (compose/union/intersection/difference/symmetric_difference) return wrong graph class; compose/union also drop attrs","description":"Differential testing against upstream nx turned up a broad defect
in graph operators. Two distinct bugs share the same root cause
(Rust binding always returns plain undirected ``Graph``, regardless
of input class and ignoring per-edge/per-node attrs).

**Defect 1: all 5 binary operators return plain Graph on DiGraph / Multi*Graph inputs.**

    import franken_networkx as fnx, networkx as nx

    dg1 = fnx.DiGraph(); dg1.add_edge('a', 'b')
    dg2 = fnx.DiGraph(); dg2.add_edge('c', 'd')

    # All of these should be DiGraph but return plain Graph:
    assert isinstance(fnx.compose(dg1, dg2), fnx.DiGraph)              # FAILS
    assert isinstance(fnx.union(dg1, dg2), fnx.DiGraph)                # FAILS
    assert isinstance(fnx.intersection(dg1, dg1), fnx.DiGraph)         # FAILS
    assert isinstance(fnx.symmetric_difference(dg1, dg1), fnx.DiGraph) # FAILS
    assert isinstance(fnx.difference(dg1, dg1), fnx.DiGraph)           # FAILS

    mg1 = fnx.MultiGraph(); mg1.add_edge(0, 1, key=0)
    mg2 = fnx.MultiGraph(); mg2.add_edge(2, 3, key=0)
    assert isinstance(fnx.union(mg1, mg2), fnx.MultiGraph)             # FAILS
    assert isinstance(fnx.intersection(mg1, mg1), fnx.MultiGraph)      # FAILS

**Defect 2: compose + union drop edge and node attribute dicts.**

    gn = nx.Graph(); gn.add_node(0, color='red'); gn.add_edge(0, 1, weight=5.0)
    gf = fnx.Graph(); gf.add_node(0, color='red'); gf.add_edge(0, 1, weight=5.0)
    hn = nx.Graph(); hn.add_edge(2, 3, weight=7.0)
    hf = fnx.Graph(); hf.add_edge(2, 3, weight=7.0)

    # nx preserves node.data and edge.data; fnx returns all empty dicts.
    assert [d for _, d in nx.compose(gn, hn).nodes(data=True)] == [{'color':'red'}, {}, {}, {}]
    assert [d for _, d in fnx.compose(gf, hf).nodes(data=True)] == [{}, {}, {}, {}]  # FAIL

(Same for ``fnx.union``. ``intersection``/``difference``/``symmetric_difference``
correctly drop attrs, matching nx by design.)

**Defect 3: fnx.compose on MultiGraph raises unrelated TypeError.**

    mg1 = fnx.MultiGraph(); mg1.add_edge(0, 1, key=0)
    mg2 = fnx.MultiGraph(); mg2.add_edge(1, 2, key=0)
    fnx.compose(mg1, mg2)
    # -> TypeError: __call__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'keys'

The error originates in the EdgeView wrapper's ``_edge_view_call_with_nbunch_first``
helper (python/franken_networkx/__init__.py:191) which accepts only
``nbunch`` / ``data`` / ``default`` kwargs and rejects ``keys=True``
that the compose internals pass for multigraphs.

**Impact:**

- Drop-in-replacement contract (README line 7) is broken at two
  layers: type (Graph vs DiGraph/MultiGraph) and data (attrs).
- Chain-of-algorithms failures: anyone doing
  ``fnx.pagerank(fnx.compose(A, B))`` on directed inputs gets a
  silently-wrong answer because the undirected Graph now lacks
  outgoing-vs-incoming semantics.
- MultiGraph compose is outright broken (crashes).

**Fix plan:**

1. Rust binding (``fnx-algorithms`` or wherever ``_raw_union``,
   ``_raw_compose``, ``_raw_intersection``, ``_raw_difference``,
   ``_raw_symmetric_difference`` live) — detect input graph class
   and build output of the same class. Node-attr and edge-attr
   dicts must be carried through for compose + union.
2. If the Rust fix is deferred, the Python wrappers can rebuild
   types + attrs by inspecting the inputs after dispatch (the
   pattern already used in ``_union_with_rename_via_parity`` from
   franken_networkx-3ehfp).
3. Multigraph-compose crash: either fix the EdgeView wrapper to
   accept ``keys=`` (preferred), or route multigraph inputs through
   a Python path that iterates via ``self.edges(keys=True, data=True)``
   explicitly.
4. Regression tests: add
   ``tests/python/test_operators_conformance.py`` with one test per
   operator × each input class combination, asserting isinstance
   and attribute round-trip (where the nx contract preserves attrs).

**Surfaced by:** /testing-conformance-harnesses differential scan on
graph operators. The existing beads queue had none of these —
covered by parity tests on the public surface but never on the
wire-level return-type contract.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:42:02.026555101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:42:02.026555101Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-8sga","title":"parity: conversion live views simple EdgeView should satisfy mapping protocol","description":"The top-level to_directed(Graph) and to_undirected(DiGraph) live views currently fail NetworkX's simple EdgeView mapping contract. In FrankenNetworkX, dict(view.edges) silently produces {'a': 'b'}-style payloads and view.edges.get((u, v), default) is missing, while upstream NetworkX returns {(u, v): data} mappings and supports tuple-key get(). Match the upstream simple EdgeView mapping contract on conversion live views.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:52:30.923200121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:16:50.048177455Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:16:32.821247668Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"franken_networkx-8sga","author":"ubuntu","text":"Fixed in 11ba7d2. Added __getitem__/__contains__ on _ConversionEdgeView (routed through view.adj which already carries the directionality semantics) + bound _adjacency_view_get/keys/items/values via in-class assignment (class is defined after the helpers, so inline binding avoids forward-ref ordering issues). dict(view.edges) now produces {(u,v): attrs}, get((u,v), default) works, and __contains__ is tuple-aware. New test_conversion_live_view_edges_satisfies_mapping_protocol parametrized over to_directed(Graph) and to_undirected(DiGraph).","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:16:50Z"}]}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-8ud1m","title":"edge_connectivity returns weighted maxflow instead of edge cardinality on weighted graphs","description":"Differential scan against upstream nx turned up a silent-incorrect bug.

``fnx.edge_connectivity(G, s, t)`` returns the **capacity-weighted max-flow value**
when G has a ``capacity`` edge attribute, rather than the **edge cardinality**
of the minimum s-t edge cut (which is the documented ``edge_connectivity``
definition and what upstream nx returns).

Reproducer::

    import franken_networkx as fnx, networkx as nx

    # Graph with capacity attrs
    g = fnx.DiGraph()
    g.add_edges_from([
        ('s','a',{'capacity':10}),
        ('s','b',{'capacity':5}),
        ('a','t',{'capacity':10}),
        ('b','t',{'capacity':10}),
        ('a','b',{'capacity':2}),
    ])
    gn = nx.DiGraph()
    gn.add_edges_from([
        ('s','a',{'capacity':10}),
        ('s','b',{'capacity':5}),
        ('a','t',{'capacity':10}),
        ('b','t',{'capacity':10}),
        ('a','b',{'capacity':2}),
    ])

    # nx returns 2 — there are 2 edges leaving s, min cut is {s-a, s-b}
    # fnx returns 15.0 — maxflow with those capacities
    assert nx.edge_connectivity(gn, 's', 't') == 2
    assert fnx.edge_connectivity(g,  's', 't') == 2   # FAILS — returns 15.0

On unweighted graphs the bug is invisible because cardinality ==
maxflow when all edges have implicit capacity 1. It only manifests
when the caller has already put ``capacity`` attrs on edges for
flow algorithms.

Why this matters:

- ``edge_connectivity`` is a structural graph property — unit of
  measurement is "number of edges", not "flow units". A graph that
  needs 2 edges cut is 2-edge-connected regardless of how heavy
  those edges are.
- Anyone computing k-edge-connectedness via ``edge_connectivity(G) >= k``
  on a weighted graph silently gets wrong answers.
- Downstream: ``is_k_edge_connected`` and related predicates may
  also inherit this error depending on their internal dispatch.

Likely root cause:

- fnx's ``edge_connectivity`` calls the max-flow primitive and
  returns the flow value instead of reducing the graph to unit
  capacities (1 per edge) first.

Fix plan:

1. In fnx.edge_connectivity: strip or override the ``capacity``
   attr so every edge has capacity 1 before running the underlying
   max-flow, then return the integer cardinality.
2. Audit ``is_k_edge_connected``, ``all_node_cuts``,
   ``minimum_edge_cut`` for the same issue — minimum_edge_cut on
   the same input returns a valid cut (different tie-break from
   nx but same size), so it's probably fine, but double-check.
3. Regression test: one-line differential vs nx on a weighted
   capacity-attr graph.

Surfaced by /testing-conformance-harnesses scan of flow/cut family.
Same scan also flagged ``minimum_edge_cut`` returning a different
(but equivalent-size) cut set — that's a tie-break divergence, not
a correctness bug, so not filed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:44:19.486272472Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:44:19.486272472Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-y7qf","title":"parity: graph classes should expose dict factory attributes and multigraph key factories","description":"Graph-family classes currently lack NetworkX's dict-factory attributes adjlist_inner_dict_factory, adjlist_outer_dict_factory, edge_attr_dict_factory, graph_attr_dict_factory, node_attr_dict_factory, and node_dict_factory. MultiGraph and MultiDiGraph also lack edge_key_dict_factory and new_edge_key(). Upstream exposes all of these; the dict factories materialize empty dicts by default, and new_edge_key('a','b') returns 1 on a graph that already has key 'k1'. Match the upstream factory/helper surface across the core graph classes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:45:20.207108301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:58:49.728664011Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:58:49.726847624Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_networkx-y87ra","title":"immediate_dominators includes start node; upstream nx excludes it","description":"Minor parity divergence turned up by the /testing-conformance-harnesses
scan on DAG algorithms.

``fnx.immediate_dominators(G, start)`` includes a ``{start: start}``
entry in the returned dict. Upstream ``nx.immediate_dominators``
excludes the start node per its documented contract (see nx
docstring quoted below).

Reproducer::

    import franken_networkx as fnx, networkx as nx

    gn = nx.DiGraph(); gn.add_edges_from([(0,1),(0,2),(1,3),(2,3),(3,4),(3,5)])
    gf = fnx.DiGraph(); gf.add_edges_from([(0,1),(0,2),(1,3),(2,3),(3,4),(3,5)])

    # nx  -> {1: 0, 2: 0, 3: 0, 4: 3, 5: 3}        (no key 0)
    # fnx -> {0: 0, 1: 0, 2: 0, 3: 0, 4: 3, 5: 3}  (includes 0 -> 0)
    assert fnx.immediate_dominators(gf, 0) == nx.immediate_dominators(gn, 0)  # FAILS

Upstream nx's docstring (verbatim)::

    idom : dict keyed by nodes
        A dict containing the immediate dominators of each node reachable from
        `start`, **except for `start` itself**.

Impact:

- Parity tests that do ``dict(fnx.immediate_dominators(G, s)) ==
  dict(nx.immediate_dominators(G, s))`` fail on the start-key
  presence.
- Users iterating ``.items()`` get an unexpected ``(start, start)``
  pair that their nx code wouldn't have produced. Practical effect
  depends on downstream code; most uses are robust to the extra
  entry because ``idom[start] == start`` is a self-loop and
  dominator-tree construction typically filters self-dominators.

Note: the existing fnx test at
``tests/python/test_dag_additional.py::TestImmediateDominators::test_chain``
explicitly asserts ``idom["a"] == "a"`` (the start-included shape),
so that test encodes the divergence and would need an update in
lockstep with the fix.

Fix plan:

1. ``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py::immediate_dominators`` (or
   wherever it dispatches from _fnx) — drop the ``{start: start}``
   entry from the returned dict before returning.
2. Update ``test_dag_additional.py::test_chain``, ``test_diamond``,
   etc. to no longer assert on ``idom[root]``.
3. Regression test: differential against ``nx.immediate_dominators``
   on a small DAG + start pair.

Priority: P3 — semantic divergence but inclusion is a superset of
the nx output (no dominator value is wrong, just an extra self-loop
entry).

Surfaced by /testing-conformance-harnesses scan; one of ~35 probe
calls across the DAG family, no other DAG divergences (the
topological_sort / dag_longest_path mismatches are legitimate
